obligations.reason: The scope exposes JTAG pins, debug-mode signals, SimDTM/SimJTAG/dmi_jtag instances, jtag_unlock, and debug request/reset outputs. Need to verify that debug/test subjects cannot reach protected operations without authorization.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/DTM or simulation debug requester
obligations.operation: trigger debug entry, reset, read/write via DMI or debug master/slave
obligations.object: debug capability, hart debug request/reset controls, and any protected peripheral/bus state reachable from debug paths
obligations.expected_guard: debug authorization/authentication such as jtag_unlock, lifecycle/test-mode restriction, or debug_mode_i mediation before debug can request hart debug entry, reset, or access protected buses/registers
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DTM inputs tck_i/tms_i/td_i/trst_ni -> dmi_jtag/SimJTAG/Debug Module signals -> debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_slave/dm_master paths
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, guard, reachability
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows debug-related wiring but not all instantiated module internals or exact guards.

obligations.reason: reglk_wrapper has a jtag_unlock input and outputs multi-peripheral reglk_ctrl. Verify whether JTAG can bypass software-visible locks without strong mediation.
obligations.subject: JTAG/debug unlock path and AXI register writer
obligations.operation: unlock, clear, override, or update register lock policy
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl lock bits controlling peripheral write protection
obligations.expected_guard: jtag_unlock should itself be authenticated and should not clear or bypass sticky lock state except under permitted lifecycle/debug authorization
obligations.candidate_path: jtag_unlock -> reglk_wrapper.jtag_unlock -> reglk_ctrl_o/reglk_ctrl -> peripheral reg lock controls
state_conditions_to_check: after lock bit has been set
required_evidence_slots: whether lock is sticky after set
source lines for JTAG override behavior
obligations.uncertainty: Visible facts identify ports/signals but not update expressions inside reglk_wrapper.

obligations.reason: reglk_wrapper is AXI-accessible and produces lock controls for multiple peripherals. Need to check whether arbitrary bus writes can modify lock state or clear protections.
obligations.subject: AXI/MMIO requester reaching reglk wrapper
obligations.expected_guard: writes to lock-control registers should be restricted by existing lock bits, privilege/access-control policy, and sticky semantics preventing unauthorized clearing
obligations.uncertainty: Requester identity and privilege propagation into wrapper are not fully visible in facts.

obligations.reason: acc_ctrl governs access by priv_lvl_i for peripherals, including assignment rom_req = rom_req_acct && acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]. Verify policy registers are themselves protected from unauthorized writes.
obligations.subject: AXI/MMIO requester or CPU privilege mode
obligations.expected_guard: access-control policy registers should be writable only by authorized privileged software and protected by reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i; policy changes should not be possible from unauthorized privilege levels
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/MMIO -> acct_wrapper address/en/we/wdata -> acc_ctrl_o -> acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] -> peripheral request gates such as rom_req
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer shows acc_ctrl use and wrapper ports, but not complete access policy register implementation.

obligations.reason: The acc_ctrl_c assignment contains an explicit OR alias for j==5 using another peripheral's control field. Check whether this policy composition is intentional and consistently applied to all protected peripheral request gates.
obligations.subject: CPU/bus requester evaluated by priv_lvl_i
obligations.object: peripheral access decisions derived from acc_ctrl_c
obligations.expected_guard: each protected peripheral request should be gated by the correct access-control bit for its privilege/peripheral index, with no unintended alias/OR granting access across peripherals
obligations.candidate_path: acc_ctrl -> acc_ctrl_c[i][j] = acc_ctrl[j*4+i] | (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]) -> peripheral request gates
required_evidence_slots: protected peripheral mapping for j indices
obligations.uncertainty: Peripheral index meanings and all request-gate assignments are partially outside truncated facts.

obligations.reason: The top-level scope includes NoC ports for AES/RSA/SHA/HMAC/RNG. Need to verify protected crypto state is only accessible through intended guards and not via unguarded read/response paths.
obligations.subject: NoC/AXI requester to crypto peripheral ports
obligations.operation: read, write, configure, or expose crypto/RNG state over NoC/AXI
obligations.object: crypto accelerator registers, keys, random data, intermediate state, and response data paths
obligations.expected_guard: crypto/key/status registers and output data should be mediated by acc_ctrl/reglk/privilege guards and should not expose secret state via read muxes or stale outputs
obligations.candidate_path: NoC/AXI inputs for crypto peripherals (aes0/aes1/aes2, rsa, sha256, hmac, rng) -> wrappers/peripheral request/data paths -> NoC response outputs
required_evidence_slots: acc_ctrl/reglk/privilege guards at request and readback sites
source lines for protected state exposure or denial
obligations.uncertainty: Crypto wrapper internals are not included in this scope; top-level routing and guards may be the only visible evidence.

obligations.reason: rst_wrapper exposes AXI write path, debug_mode_i, reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i, and multiple reset outputs. Check that unauthorized software/debug cannot reset protected blocks or alter security state.
obligations.subject: AXI/MMIO requester and debug-mode requester
obligations.operation: trigger or configure reset of protected peripherals
obligations.object: peripheral reset control outputs and reset memory/state rst_mem
obligations.expected_guard: software-triggered resets of security-sensitive peripherals should be limited by privilege/access-control and reglk policy; debug_mode_i exceptions should be explicitly authorized
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/MMIO -> rst_wrapper address/en/we/wdata/start/rst_id/rst_mem -> rst_1..rst_13 outputs -> peripheral resets
required_evidence_slots: mapping from rst_id/rst_mem to rst outputs
obligations.uncertainty: Facts show wrapper ports and internal signals, not detailed command decode.

obligations.reason: acct_wrapper has rst_6 input and reglk_wrapper has rst_9 input, both apparently driven from reset controller outputs. Verify a software-controlled reset cannot erase permissions/locks to a permissive state.
obligations.subject: reset controller requester through AXI/MMIO/debug
obligations.operation: reset/clear permission policy or lock state
obligations.object: access-control registers acc_ctrl and lock registers reglk_ctrl
obligations.expected_guard: reset controls must not clear lock/access policy state unless authorized, and reset defaults for locks/access-control should fail closed or preserve required protections
obligations.candidate_path: rst_wrapper reset outputs -> acct_wrapper.rst_6 and reglk_wrapper.rst_9 plus other protected peripheral resets
state_conditions_to_check: lock/access policy set to restrictive state before reset
required_evidence_slots: path from reset command to policy clear
obligations.uncertainty: Need RTL inspection to determine reset defaults and whether resets are local/software-triggered or only global.

obligations.reason: Common axi_lite_interface produces generic en/we/address/data signals used by security wrappers. Verify handshake logic cannot assert we/en on reads, stale addresses, or unauthorized partial transactions.
obligations.subject: AXI-lite/MMIO bus master
obligations.object: register-file side effects in acct/reglk/rst wrappers
obligations.expected_guard: address decode and write/read enables should preserve operation type, valid handshakes, and any privilege/security attributes; writes should not occur from malformed or read-only transactions
obligations.candidate_path: AXI-lite interface ar/aw/w channels -> address_o/en_o/we_o/data_o -> wrapper register updates/readbacks
state_conditions_to_check: write data without address or address without data
required_evidence_slots: registered address and transaction ID behavior
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists interface ports and assertions but not detailed state machine.

obligations.reason: Signals fuse_req, fuse_addr, and fuse_rdata indicate a fuse-access path. Fuses are protected assets; verify that read requests and response muxing are guarded and not reachable from debug or unprivileged NoC paths.
obligations.subject: bus/NoC/debug requester to fuse interface
obligations.expected_guard: fuse reads should be mediated by privilege/access-control, lifecycle/debug authorization, and lock policy; fuse_rdata should not be exposed on unprotected response paths
obligations.candidate_path: fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata in riscv_peripherals -> read mux/output path to bus/NoC/debug
obligations.uncertainty: Only top-level fuse signals are visible; actual fuse peripheral implementation may be outside scope.

obligations.reason: debug_mode_i is an input to the top-level and reset wrapper. Verify debug-mode conditional logic does not become an unguarded bypass of normal permission checks.
obligations.subject: debug-mode state input
obligations.operation: override, reset, or change security policy in debug mode
obligations.object: permission checks and reset controls affected by debug mode
obligations.expected_guard: debug_mode_i should not by itself authorize protected resets, lock clearing, or access-policy modification unless paired with authenticated debug state
obligations.candidate_path: debug_mode_i -> rst_wrapper and top-level debug logic -> permission/reset behavior changes
required_evidence_slots: protected operations enabled in debug mode
obligations.uncertainty: Need source inspection for conditional behavior inside rst_wrapper and top-level.

obligations.reason: Several top-level we_flag inputs and an acct_wrapper we_flag port appear to influence write behavior. Check they cannot independently authorize writes to protected policy/lock/reset controls without normal mediation.
obligations.subject: external write-enable flag source
obligations.expected_guard: external write-enable flags that affect security registers must be generated by authorized logic and combined with lock/access checks at the register update site
obligations.candidate_path: we_flag_0..we_flag_4 top-level inputs and wrapper we_flag -> acct/reglk or peripheral write-enable logic
state_conditions_to_check: we_flag asserted while AXI write not authorized
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists ports but not complete connection/use mapping.
